<insights>

- High coupling (0.52) — 7 imports target "autonomous-agent-engine"
- The zone groups read-only CLI commands (show, status, task-lookup) with their shared constants, forming a clean display layer that never mutates agent state.
- task-lookup.ts bridges CLI string input to PRD task resolution, acting as a translation layer between raw CLI arguments and domain objects — its 7 inbound imports from hench-unit reflect this adaptor role.
- Test coverage includes the deleted-task edge case, indicating this layer explicitly handles domain failure modes at the CLI boundary.
- The 7 cross-zone imports from hench-cli into hench-unit are appropriate for a read-only CLI adaptor that delegates all domain logic to the agent engine zone.
- Moderate cohesion (0.48) is expected for a CLI command zone where commands share constants but each command has an independent control path.
- The 'run' command for hench likely lives in the Autonomous Agent Engine zone, creating an asymmetry between the read (CLI commands here) and write (run command there) surfaces — document this split intentionally.
